_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to analyse and evaluate how language features are used to explicitly represent values, beliefs and attitudes in texts.

_source.w: We are learning to analyse and evaluate how language features are used to explicitly represent values, beliefs and attitudes in texts.
y: Every text embodies values — being able to identify and evaluate them is essential critical literacy.
This skill helps us recognise how texts attempt to shape our thinking and beliefs.
Understanding how values are encoded in language empowers us to make informed judgments about the texts we encounter.
eg: We analyse how particular vocabulary choices in a text explicitly signal the author's values and attitudes.
We evaluate how language features like modality, evaluative language and intensifiers represent beliefs.
We compare texts that represent different value positions on the same issue and identify the language that marks those positions.
We discuss how texts can simultaneously express and promote particular values.
We identify the language features in our own writing that reveal our values and consider whether this is intentional.
We write a critical analysis of how a text uses language to represent specific values and attitudes.
